title: Pachyphytum cuicatecanum
taxonomy: Pachyphytum cuicatecanum is classified as a species of succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae. This family is commonly known as the stonecrop or orpine family. Within the genus Pachyphytum, Pachyphytum cuicatecanum is one of the many species, which are native to Mexico. The classification of Pachyphytum cuicatecanum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Pachyphytum cuicatecanum is a part of the class Magnoliopsida, which is a class of flowering plants.

intro: Pachyphytum cuicatecanum, commonly known as Cuicatec Pachyphytum, is a succulent plant species native to Mexico, characterized by its fleshy, blue-green leaves and short stems. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can provide relief from congestion and promote a healthy immune system. The bioactive constituents of Pachyphytum cuicatecanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Mexican medicine, the plant is commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
